# Attention, Not Self

> A personal inquiry into Buddhist Abhidharma and computational phenomenology — mapping ancient dharma classification systems (Theravāda, Sarvāstivāda, Yogācāra) onto modern computational models of attention, consciousness, and cognition. Project namesake: Jonardon Ganeri, *Attention, Not Self* (Oxford 2017).

## Core documentation

- [Full AI Reference](llms-full.txt): self-contained FAQ, glossary, and prior research references for AI agents
- [README (English)](README.en.md): human-facing project overview
- [README (Japanese, canonical)](README.md): 日本語版プロジェクト正本

## Essays — Theme 1: Abhidharma meets computational phenomenology (introduction)

Introductory mapping between Abhidharma and computational phenomenology — predictive processing, active inference, neurophenomenology. Strongest correspondence: *manaskāra* (attention) ↔ precision-weighting.

- [Abhidharma Meets Computational Phenomenology](Abhidharma%20Meets%20Computational%20Phenomenology.md): English canonical version.
- [アビダルマと計算論的現象学（日本語）](アビダルマと計算論的現象学（日本語）.md): Japanese mirror.

## Essays — Theme 2: Dharma classification across the three Abhidharma systems

Side-by-side comparison of dharma taxonomies — Theravāda (82 dhammas), Sarvāstivāda (75 dharmas), Yogācāra (100 dharmas).

- [Three Abhidharma Systems Dharma Comparison](Three%20Abhidharma%20Systems%20Dharma%20Comparison.md): English canonical version.
- [三大アビダルマ法対照表（日本語）](三大アビダルマ法対照表（日本語）.md): Japanese mirror.

## Essays — Theme 3: Cognitive process models in the three Abhidharma traditions

Comparative study of how the three Abhidharma traditions explain cognition from stimulus to conscious experience — Theravāda's 17-moment *citta-vīthi*, Sarvāstivāda's *saṃprayoga* + *samanantara-pratyaya* + three-time realism, Yogācāra's four-*bhāga* + *ālaya-vijñāna* architecture.

- [Three Abhidharma Models of Cognition](Three%20Abhidharma%20Models%20of%20Cognition.md): English canonical version.
- [三大アビダルマ伝統における認識過程の比較研究（日本語）](三大アビダルマ伝統における認識過程の比較研究（日本語）.md): Japanese mirror.

## Essays — Theme 4: Mapping cognitive process models onto computational phenomenology

Systematic mapping of the three Abhidharma cognitive process architectures onto computational frameworks — Theravāda *citta-vīthi* ↔ Global Workspace Theory, Sarvāstivāda *saṃprayoga* ↔ Parallel Distributed Processing, Yogācāra *ālaya-vijñāna* ↔ predictive processing / active inference. Includes best-fit table, evidence-quality summary, and the hard limits (karmic valence, *nirvāṇa*) that resist any computational analog.

- [Abhidharma Cognitive Process Models Meets Computational Phenomenology](Abhidharma%20Cognitive%20Process%20Models%20Meets%20Computational%20Phenomenology.md): English canonical version.
- [三大アビダルマ認識過程モデルと計算論的現象学（日本語）](三大アビダルマ認識過程モデルと計算論的現象学（日本語）.md): Japanese mirror.

## Essays — Theme 5: Genealogy and typology of "awareness" / 気づき

Unpacks the Buddhist technical vocabulary collapsed into the single word "awareness" / 気づき — *sati*, *sampajañña*, *manasikāra*, *āvajjana*, *viññāṇa*, *paññā*, *rigpa*, *appamāda*, *jāgariyā*, *āloka*. Traces the Western genealogy (Rhys Davids 1881 → Theosophy → D. T. Suzuki → Nyanaponika's "bare attention" 1962 → Ram Dass / Watts / Trungpa → Perls / Esalen / est / NLP → Kabat-Zinn's MBSR 1979 → Sam Harris's *rigpa*-inflected app) and the Japanese trajectory (Thich Nhat Hanh translations → MBSR import → 自己啓発 / coaching / NLP industries). Typologizes ten resulting confusions including the *sati*–*rigpa* conflation, the active–passive paradox, and the phenomenological–ontological slide.

- [Awareness - A Genealogy and Typology](Awareness%20-%20A%20Genealogy%20and%20Typology.md): English canonical version.
- [気づきとAwareness - 系譜と類型論（日本語）](気づきとAwareness%20-%20系譜と類型論（日本語）.md): Japanese mirror.

## Comparison tables (data)

- [TABLES.md](TABLES.md): Browser-readable Markdown rendering of the dharma comparison spreadsheet — recommended for direct reading on GitHub
- [csv/](csv/): Per-sheet CSV exports — reusable in Excel / Numbers / pandas / R
- 三大アビダルマ法対照表.xlsx: Source spreadsheet with formatting and color coding

## Related

- [Ganeri, *Attention, Not Self* (Oxford 2017)](https://global.oup.com/academic/product/attention-not-self-9780198798507): project namesake; attention-first reading of Buddhaghosa's Abhidhamma.
